document.title: Citizen's Charter - DICT Regional Office (External Services)

services.description: The Philippine National Public Key Infrastructure (PNPKI) allows the use of digital certificates which enables secure communications among individuals and government agencies. The PNPKI issues two (2) types of digital certificates to individuals and/or organizations: (a) signing certificate, which is used for document signing and (b) authentication certificate, which is typically used for email signing and encryption.
services.office_or_division: DICT Regional Office, Regional Offices, Cybersecurity Bureau - Digital Certificate Division

for_individual_certificate.checklist_of_requirements:
1. Registration via PNPKI Online Registration System (ORS) or PNPKI Individual Certificate Application Form (for renewal and bulk applications)

(1) Original Copy Or (1) Electronic Copy
2. E-mail address owned by the individual or authorized by the owner for use by the subscriber

3. Valid Government-issued Identification




Philippine National ID (PhilID) (soft copy / scanned copy in PDF or JPG format) — Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)
or combination of the following:
— Birth Certificate or valid Philippine passport (soft copy / scanned copy in PDF or JPG) — PSA / DFA
— AND — UMID Card (soft copy / scanned copy in PDF or JPG) — SSS



(In the absence of UMID-compliant card, ANY TWO of the following cards are allowed as valid IDs based on BSP Circular 608 series of 2008)
Passport • Driver's License • PRC ID • NBI Clearance • Police Clearance • Postal ID • Voter's ID • GSIS e-Card • SSS Card • Senior Citizen Card • OWWA/OFW ID • Seaman's Book • Alien Certification of Registration / Immigrant Certificate of Registration • Government Office and GOCC ID
4. One (1) passport size photo taken within the last six (6) months

for_agency_certificate.checklist_of_requirements:
1. PNPKI Agency Certificate Application Form
(1) Original Copy
Remarks: Obtain via the DICT Website: https://dict.gov.ph/

2. Valid Government-issued Identification
Philippine National ID (PhilID) (soft copy / scanned copy in PDF or JPG) — PSA
or combination of:
— Birth Certificate (on security paper for Filipino) or ACR Card (for foreigner) or valid passport — PSA / DFA
— AND — UMID Card — SSS

(In absence of UMID-compliant card, ANY TWO of: Passport • Driver's License • PRC ID • NBI Clearance • Police Clearance • Postal ID • Voter's ID • GSIS e-Card • SSS Card • Senior Citizen Card • OWWA/OFW ID • Seaman's Book • Alien Certification of Registration / Immigrant Certificate of Registration)
3. One (1) passport size photo taken within the last six (6) months

5. Authorization Letter / Board Resolution naming the authorized representative/s to apply for a digital certificate in behalf of the agency
(1) Original Copy
6. Consent to verify the information submitted
7. The process is done via Zoom or Google

additional_for_non_government_entities.checklist_of_requirements: 1. SEC business registration for corporation and partnership, DTI Certificate of Business Name Registration for single proprietorship OR CDA registration

process_flow.client_steps:
1. Register via the Online Registration System (ORS) and submit all requirements.
Location: Home-based or via the DICT Provincial or Regional Office
Notes: Access the PNPKI ORS portal thru: https://bit.ly/ApplyPNPKI
process_flow.agency_action: 1.1. Receive and evaluate the registration and supporting documents of the applicant
process_flow.person_responsible: Registration Authority Officer (RAO)

process_flow.agency_action: 1.2. Scheduling of online verification.
process_flow.person_responsible: Registration Authority Officer (RAO)

process_flow.agency_action: 1.3. Conduct of video call with the applicant through video conferencing platforms such as: Viber, Zoom, and Hangouts
process_flow.person_responsible:
Registration Authority Officer (RAO);
Digital Certificate Division PNPKI Support Team / Assigned Personnel

process_flow.agency_action: 1.4. Adding user (including verification and add user into TMSRA from ORS of the applicant) into the PNPKI System.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Registration Authority Assistants (RAA);
Digital Certificate Division PNPKI Support Team / Assigned Personnel

process_flow.agency_action: 1.5. Once the add user request is approved by another RA Officer (RAO), the system sends an email to the subscriber with instructions on how to download and install digital certificates.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Registration Authority Assistants (RAA);
Digital Certificate Division PNPKI Support Team / Assigned Personnel

process_flow.client_steps:
2. Request for digital certificates.
Location: Home-based or via the DICT Provincial or Regional Office
process_flow.agency_action: 2. Approve certificate request.
process_flow.person_responsible: Registration Authority Officer (RAO)

process_flow.client_steps:
3. Download and install digital certificates.
Location: Home-based or via the DICT Provincial or Regional Office
process_flow.agency_action: 3. Digital Certificate Division PNPKI Support Team / assigned personnel, provides support and assistance as needed.
process_flow.person_responsible: Digital Certificate Division PNPKI Support Team / Assigned Personnel

services.service_name: Processing of Application to Take the ICT Diagnostic Exam
services.description: The Philippine National Public Key Infrastructure (PNPKI) allows the use of digital certificates which enables secure communications among individuals and government agencies. The PNPKI issues two (2) types of digital certificates: (a) signing certificate, which is used for document signing and (b) authentication certificate, which is typically used for email signing and encryption. This way, the government's delivery of services to citizens and businesses becomes safer, faster and more efficient.
services.office_or_division: DICT Regional Office, Regional Offices, ICT Literacy and Competency Development Bureau

checklist_of_requirements.checklist_of_requirements:
Accomplished Application Form

https://tinyurl.com/DiagExamRegForm

https://tinyurl.com/DiagExamRegForm

process_flow.person_responsible:

ICT Literacy and Competency Development Division (ILCDB)

services.service_name: Issuance of Verified ICT Diagnostic Examination Results
services.description: The ICT Proficiency Certification Examination is designed to evaluate the competence of an individual to perform programming or systems analysis and design functions. This is pursuant to PD 1408, which directs the National Computer Center (now DICT) and the Civil Service Commission (CSC) to devise ways of determining the fitness of individuals in occupying highly technical ICT positions. The ICT Diagnostic Exam is a prerequisite for the ICT Proficiency Hands on Exam.
services.office_or_division: DICT Regional Office, Regional Offices, ICT Literacy and Competency Development Bureau

Accomplished Application Form

ICT Literacy and Competency Development Division (ILCDB)

services.service_name: Processing of Application to Take the ICT Proficiency Hands on Examination
services.description: The ICT Proficiency Certification Examination is designed to validate the competence of an individual in programming and system analysis and design functions. This is pursuant to PD 1408, which directs the National Computer Center (now DICT) and the Civil Service Commission (CSC) to devise ways of determining the fitness of individuals in highly technical ICT positions. DICT and CSC therefore conduct tests in these areas.
services.office_or_division: DICT Regional Office, Regional Offices, ICT Literacy and Competency Development Bureau

checklist_of_requirements.checklist_of_requirements:
1. Accomplished Application Form
(1) Electronic Copy
Remarks: Access the application form through this link: https://tinyurl.com/HandsOnExamRegForm

3. Transcript of Records (TOR)
(1) Electronic Copy And (1) Photo Copy

4. Original Birth Certificate (NSO or PSA)
(1) Electronic Copy And (1) Original Copy
checklist_of_requirements.where_to_secure: Philippine Statistics Authority — Regional Statistical Services Office (RSSO)

checklist_of_requirements.checklist_of_requirements:
5. Passport Size ID Picture
(1) Electronic Copy And (1) Original Copy
Remarks: With handwritten name tag containing: (LAST NAME, FIRST NAME MI)

process_flow.client_steps:
1. Accomplish the Online Registration Form and submit the required documents.
Location: Access the form through the link:
https://tinyurl.com/HandsOnExamRegForm
process_flow.agency_action: 1.1 Review the Online Registration Form and its attached documentary requirements.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Training Specialist III, Training Management Division;
ICT Literacy and Competency Development Division (ILCDB)

process_flow.agency_action: 1.2 Send confirmation of application and request for submission of physical documentary requirements.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Training Specialist III, Training Management Division;
ILCDB

process_flow.client_steps:
2. Receive confirmation of application and submit physical documentary requirements.
Location: An auto-reply will be displayed upon completing the form. Another confirmation email will be sent through ilcdb.registrar@dict.gov.ph for instructions on submitting physical copies.
process_flow.agency_action: 2.1 Review the submitted documents.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Training Specialist III, Training Management Division;
ICT Literacy and Competency Development Division (ILCDB)

process_flow.agency_action: 2.2 Prepare the Order of Payment Form
process_flow.person_responsible:
Preparation: Registrar's Office Staff
Signing: Accounting Head

process_flow.client_steps:
3. Receive Order of Payment Form and pay the necessary fees.
Location: Ground Floor, DICT Building, C.P. Garcia Avenue, Diliman, Quezon City
process_flow.agency_action: 3.1 Issue signed Order of Payment

For Professionals: PHP 500
For Graduating Students: PHP 300
process_flow.person_responsible:
Chief Administrative Officer;
Accounting Division
Supervising Administrative Officer;
Accounting Division

process_flow.agency_action: 3.2 Receive payment and issue Official Receipt (OR)
process_flow.person_responsible:
Chief Administrative Officer;
Cash Division
Supervising Administrative Officer;
Cash Division

process_flow.client_steps:
4. Receive the OR from the Cash Division and present the same to the ILCDB.
Location: Registrar's Office, 2nd Floor, DICT Valenzuela, Karuhatan, Valenzuela City
process_flow.agency_action: 4.1 Validate the OR and issue the Examination Permit.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Training Specialist III, Training Management Division;
ICT Literacy and Competency Development Division (ILCDB)

via DICT Cashier
Location: G/F DICT Bldg., C.P. Garcia Avenue, Diliman, Quezon City

services.description: The ICT Proficiency Certification Examination is designed to validate the competence of an individual in programming and system analysis and design functions. This is pursuant to PD 1408, which directs the National Computer Center (now DICT) and the Civil Service Commission (CSC) to devise ways of determining the fitness of individuals in highly technical ICT positions. DICT and CSC therefore conduct tests in these areas.

services.service_name: Processing of Request for ICT Technical Assistance and Services
services.description: The Regional Office provides ICT-related technical assistance to LGUs and SUCs, such as provisioning of online meeting platforms, the conduct of training sessions and webinars, and review of Information Systems Strategic Plan, among others.

process_flow.client_steps:
1. Forward the request letter indicating the specific technical assistance needed via e-mail to the respective regional office email.
Location: Official Regional Emails
process_flow.agency_action: 1.1. Review and Evaluate Online Application Form and Requirements
process_flow.person_responsible:
Provincial Officers / Project Focal
Provincial / Regional Office

process_flow.agency_action: 1.2. Forward the request letter to the office concerned
process_flow.person_responsible:
Provincial Officers / Project Focal
Provincial / Regional Office

process_flow.agency_action: 1.3. The office concerned evaluates if the request classifies as Simple, Complex and Highly Technical.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Provincial Officers / Project Focal
Provincial / Regional Office

process_flow.agency_action: 1.4. Endorse the recommendation to the Provincial Officer or Regional Director for approval.
process_flow.person_responsible:
Project Focal
Technical Operations Division

process_flow.client_steps: 2. Receive the letter of decision whether approved or disapproved.
process_flow.agency_action: 2. Send a reply letter
process_flow.person_responsible:
Project Focal
Technical Operations Division

how_to_send_feedback: Accomplish our Feedback Form available in the offices and put this at the drop box located at the Public Assistance and Complaints Desk
Feedback may also be sent through e-mail feedback@dict.gov.ph or consumer.protection@dict.gov.ph
Talk to our Officer of the Day
how_feedbacks_are_processed: Client files feedback either through accomplishing Feedback Form available in the lobby or by sending their feedback through e-mail address: feedback@dict.gov.ph
Personnel assigned as Officer of the Day records, reviews the feedback and forwards this to appropriate Services/Unit of the Office.
All feedback recorded for the day will be submitted to the Committee on Anti-Red Tape (CART) Secretariat.
The CART Secretariat shall ensure that proper acknowledgment shall be given to those who filed their feedback within fifteen (15) days upon receipt.

The CART Secretariat shall prepare a monthly summary of feedbacks received and forward the report to the CART Chairperson or Supervising Senior Executive Official/s for Central Office and Office of the Regional Director for Regional Offices.
Feedback inputs received from the Officer of the Day will be used as a reference for process/product/service improvements.

how_to_file_a_complaint.channel: For service-related complaints against DICT, email Anti-Red Tape Authority (ARTA) through complaints@arta.gov.ph
required_details: Full name and contact information of the complainant
Name of the person/office being complained

how_complaints_are_processed: The DICT Committee on Anti Red Tape (CART) evaluates all complaints against DICT filed through ARTA, and identifies if the same is within DICT jurisdiction, mandate and/or authority.

Upon evaluation, the CART shall start the investigation and forward the complaint to the relevant office for their explanation. The concerned office should reply within 3 days upon receipt of the referred complaint from CART.

The CART shall create a CART Referral Report or an equivalent report and return the complete docket case to the Public Assistance Division of ARTA within 20 working days. The 20 day period may be extended once for the same period through a written consent of the complainant.
If successfully resolved: The CART shall cause the complainant to file the Consent to Closure Complaint (Annex C of ARTA MC No. 2021-11).

If failed to resolve: The CART shall submit the CART Referral Report together with: (1) Sworn Affidavit of the Complainant, (2) Counter-Affidavit of the Complained Government Official, (3) Recommendation of the CART Chairperson or head of DICT.
